mission.title: spire: Smarter Authorized Entry Caching
mission.description: Smarter Authorized Entry Caching. Requested by 6+ users.

steps.title: Check current spire deployment
steps.description:
Verify your spire version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n spire -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire
helm list -n spire 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working spire installation.

steps.title: Review spire configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant spire configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n spire -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire
kubectl get configmap -n spire -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=spire
```
In 0.12.0 we introduced a full in-memory authorized entry cache.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Smarter Authorized Entry Caching
steps.description:
Uses an event in sql to build the cache. Rather than fetch the whole table every 5 seconds, get only the events that occurred since the last fetch.

Creates a new table in the sql database and a bunch of triggers. For example when a new entry is created the trigger will create an entry in the entry events table. SPIRE will read this table and update the in memory cache.

Code to prune the event table is missing in this draft.

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n spire -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire
kubectl get events -n spire --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Smarter Authorized Entry Caching" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Uses an event in sql to build the cache. Rather than fetch the whole table every 5 seconds, get only the events that occurred since the last fetch.

Creates a new table in the sql database and a bunch of triggers. For example when a new entry is created the trigger will create an entry in the entry events table. SPIRE will read this table and update the in memory cache.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with spire installed or the issue environment reproducible.
